1	#!/usr/bin/env bash
2	#
3	# Canonical bench against the running vLLM service.
4	#   - Runs both the canonical narrative AND code prompts in one invocation.
5	#     This matches the README's narrative/code TPS pairing.
6	#   - 3 warmup + N measured runs per prompt (default 5 narrative + 5 code).
7	#   - per-run: wall time, TTFT (via streaming), completion tokens,
8	#     wall_TPS (= comp / wall), decode_TPS (= comp / (wall - TTFT))
9	#   - per-prompt summary: mean / std / CV for both TPS metrics + mean TTFT
10	#   - shows MTP SpecDecoding metrics from docker logs at the end
11	#
12	# Why two TPS metrics:
13	#   - wall_TPS  = "user-perceived speed" (includes prefill cost)
14	#   - decode_TPS = "model decode rate" (excludes prefill)
15	#   For long prompts the two can differ a lot. For short prompts they
16	#   converge. Reporting both keeps comparisons honest across configs.
17	#
18	# Why narrative + code:
19	#   MTP acceptance varies wildly by prompt structure. Code (repetitive,
20	#   token-predictable) accepts at ~80% per position; prose (semantically
21	#   rich) at ~50%. Reporting only one half is misleading. README claims
22	#   like "66 / 85 TPS" pair them; bench should too.
23	#
24	# Prereq: stack is running and reports "Application startup complete".
25	#
26	# Env vars:
27	#   URL                Endpoint. Default: http://localhost:8020
28	#   MODEL              Served model name. Default: qwen3.6-27b-autoround
29	#   CONTAINER          Container for log scraping. Default: vllm-qwen36-27b
30	#   RUNS               Measured runs per prompt. Default: 5
31	#   WARMUPS            Warm-up runs (shared across both). Default: 3
32	#   PROMPT_NARR        Override narrative prompt
33	#   PROMPT_CODE        Override code prompt
34	#   MAX_TOKENS_NARR    Default: 1000
35	#   MAX_TOKENS_CODE    Default: 800
36	#   ONLY               Set to "narr" or "code" to skip the other. Default: both
37	#   QUIET              Set to 1 to skip per-run lines (just print summary)
38	#
39	# Usage:
40	#   bash scripts/bench.sh
41	#   ONLY=code bash scripts/bench.sh
42	#   RUNS=10 bash scripts/bench.sh
43	
44	set -euo pipefail
45	
46	# Auto-detect running container + port (URL/CONTAINER env vars still win).
47	# See scripts/preflight.sh::preflight_autodetect_endpoint.
48	ROOT_DIR="$(cd -- "$(dirname -- "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}")/.." && pwd)"
49	if [[ -f "${ROOT_DIR}/scripts/preflight.sh" ]]; then

52	  preflight_autodetect_endpoint

81	import json, sys, time, urllib.request, statistics as s
82	
83	(URL, MODEL, WARMUPS, RUNS, QUIET, ONLY,
84	 PROMPT_NARR, MAX_NARR, PROMPT_CODE, MAX_CODE) = sys.argv[1:]
85	WARMUPS = int(WARMUPS); RUNS = int(RUNS); QUIET = int(QUIET) == 1
86	MAX_NARR = int(MAX_NARR); MAX_CODE = int(MAX_CODE)
87	
88	def run_once(prompt, max_tokens):
89	    body = json.dumps({
90	        "model": MODEL,
91	        "messages": [{"role": "user", "content": prompt}],
92	        "max_tokens": max_tokens,
93	        "temperature": 0.6,
94	        "top_p": 0.95,
95	        "stream": True,
96	        "stream_options": {"include_usage": True},
97	        "chat_template_kwargs": {"enable_thinking": False},
98	    }).encode()
99	    req = urllib.request.Request(f"{URL}/v1/chat/completions", data=body,
100	                                 headers={"Content-Type": "application/json"})
101	    t_send = time.time()
102	    ttft = None
103	    completion_tokens = 0
104	    with urllib.request.urlopen(req, timeout=600) as r:
105	        for line in r:
106	            line = line.decode("utf-8", errors="ignore").rstrip()
107	            if not line.startswith("data: "):
108	                continue
109	            payload = line[6:]
110	            if payload == "[DONE]":
111	                break
112	            try:
113	                chunk = json.loads(payload)
114	            except json.JSONDecodeError:
115	                continue
116	            choices = chunk.get("choices") or []
117	            if choices:
118	                delta = choices[0].get("delta", {})
119	                content = delta.get("content") or delta.get("reasoning_content")
120	                if content and ttft is None:
121	                    ttft = time.time() - t_send
122	            usage = chunk.get("usage")
123	            if usage:
124	                completion_tokens = usage.get("completion_tokens", completion_tokens)
125	    t_end = time.time()
126	    wall = t_end - t_send
127	    if ttft is None:
128	        ttft = wall
129	    return wall, ttft, completion_tokens
130	
131	def fmt(label, wall, ttft, toks):
132	    decode_t = max(wall - ttft, 1e-6)
133	    wtps = toks / wall if wall > 0 else 0
134	    dtps = toks / decode_t
135	    line = f"  {label:<10s} wall={wall:6.2f}s  ttft={ttft*1000:6.0f}ms  toks={toks:>4d}  wall_TPS={wtps:6.2f}  decode_TPS={dtps:6.2f}"
136	    return wtps, dtps, ttft, line
137	
138	def stats(name, xs, unit=""):
139	    m = s.mean(xs)
140	    sd = s.stdev(xs) if len(xs) > 1 else 0
141	    cv = (sd / m * 100) if m > 0 else 0
142	    return f"  {name:<14s} mean={m:7.2f}{unit}   std={sd:6.2f}   CV={cv:4.1f}%   min={min(xs):.2f}   max={max(xs):.2f}"
143	
144	def run_set(label, prompt, max_tokens):
145	    print(f"\n========== {label.upper()} (prompt={len(prompt)} chars, max_tokens={max_tokens}) ==========")
146	    print(f"=== warmups ({WARMUPS}) ===")
147	    for i in range(WARMUPS):
148	        try:
149	            w, t, k = run_once(prompt, max_tokens)
150	            _, _, _, line = fmt(f"warm-{i+1}", w, t, k)
151	            if not QUIET:
152	                print(line)
153	        except Exception as e:
154	            print(f"  warm-{i+1}  FAIL: {e}")
155	    print(f"\n=== measured ({RUNS}) ===")
156	    walls, decodes, ttfts = [], [], []
157	    for i in range(RUNS):
158	        try:
159	            w, t, k = run_once(prompt, max_tokens)
160	            wtps, dtps, ttft, line = fmt(f"run-{i+1}", w, t, k)
161	            if not QUIET:
162	                print(line)
163	            walls.append(wtps); decodes.append(dtps); ttfts.append(ttft)
164	        except Exception as e:
165	            print(f"  run-{i+1}  FAIL: {e}")
166	    if walls:
167	        print(f"\n=== summary [{label}] (n={len(walls)}) ===")
168	        print(stats("wall_TPS",   walls))
169	        print(stats("decode_TPS", decodes))
170	        print(f"  TTFT          mean={s.mean(ttfts)*1000:6.0f}ms  std={s.stdev(ttfts)*1000 if len(ttfts) > 1 else 0:5.0f}ms  min={min(ttfts)*1000:.0f}ms  max={max(ttfts)*1000:.0f}ms")
171	
172	if ONLY in ("both", "narr"):
173	    run_set("narrative", PROMPT_NARR, MAX_NARR)
174	if ONLY in ("both", "code"):
175	    run_set("code", PROMPT_CODE, MAX_CODE)

#!/usr/bin/env bash
#
# Switch between club-3090 compose variants.
#
# Brings down whatever's currently running, brings up the new variant,
# and (optionally) waits for the server to report ready on /v1/models.
# Stateless — re-run any time you want a different config.
#
# Usage:
#   bash scripts/switch.sh <variant>           # switch + tail until ready
#   bash scripts/switch.sh <variant> --no-wait # switch and return immediately
#   bash scripts/switch.sh --list              # show all variants
#   bash scripts/switch.sh --down              # just bring down whatever's up
#
# Variant names (engine/file, file is the docker-compose.<file>.yml stem):
#
#   Single-card vLLM:
#     vllm/default            48K + TQ3 + MTP + vision + tools (recommended)
#     vllm/long-vision        198K + TQ3 + vision (cliff-safe; Cliff 2 single-prompt >50K still applies)
#     vllm/long-text          180K + TQ3 + MTP + text-only (Balanced MTP — 60K single-prompt closed via v7.69 + #35975)
#     vllm/long-text-no-mtp   200K + TQ3 + no MTP + text-only (Max-context — same Cliff 2 closure, more KV pool, slower decode)
#     vllm/bounded-thinking   180K + TQ3 + structured-CoT FSM in reasoning (recommended grammar: DeepSeek scratchpad — 87.4% combined HE+/LCB v6)
#     vllm/tools-text         75K + fp8 + MTP + text-only (IDE agents — Cline / Cursor)
#     vllm/minimal            32K + fp8 (no Genesis, no spec-decode, simplest)
#
#   Dual-card vLLM (TP=2):
#     vllm/dual             262K + fp8 + 2 streams + vision (recommended dual)
#     vllm/dual4            262K + fp8 + 4 streams + vision (4× 3090 PCIe baseline)
#     vllm/dual4-dflash     262K + FP16 + DFlash N=5 + 2 streams + vision (4× 3090 code)
#     vllm/dual-turbo       262K + TQ3 + 4 streams + vision (multi-tenant)
#     vllm/dual-dflash      185K + FP16 + DFlash N=5 + vision (peak code TPS)
#     vllm/dual-dflash-noviz 200K + FP16 + DFlash N=5 + no vision (peak code, max ctx)
#     vllm/dual-nvlink          262K + fp8 + 2 streams + vision (REQUIRES NVLink bridge — community/experimental)
#     vllm/dual-nvlink-turbo    262K + TQ3 + 4 streams + vision (REQUIRES NVLink bridge — community/experimental)
#     vllm/dual-nvlink-dflash   185K + FP16 + DFlash N=5 + vision (REQUIRES NVLink bridge — community/experimental)
#     vllm/dual-nvlink-dflash-noviz 188K + FP16 + DFlash N=5 + no vision (REQUIRES NVLink bridge — community/experimental)
#     vllm/gemma-mtp        Gemma-4-31B + Google MTP drafter (32K, bf16 KV, vision — community/experimental, pre-merge)

271	    echo "[preflight]          If downloads fail later, regenerate at https://huggingface.co/settings/tokens" >&2
272	  fi
273	  return 0
274	}

276	# preflight_compose_deps <compose_file> — verify any model directories the compose
277	# expects to mount actually exist on the host. Catches the "you set up the repo
278	# but didn't WITH_DFLASH_DRAFT=1, then tried to launch dual-dflash-noviz" failure
279	# mode. See club-3090#37 for the canonical case.
280	#
281	# Hard error (returns 1) — refuses to proceed if a required model dir is missing.
282	# Skip via: PREFLIGHT_NO_COMPOSE_DEPS=1
283	preflight_compose_deps() {
284	  local compose_file="$1"
285	  if [[ "${PREFLIGHT_NO_COMPOSE_DEPS:-0}" == "1" ]]; then
286	    return 0
287	  fi
288	  if [[ ! -f "$compose_file" ]]; then
289	    echo "[preflight] ERROR: compose file not found: $compose_file" >&2
290	    return 1
291	  fi
292	
293	  local model_dir="${MODEL_DIR:-../../../../models-cache}"
294	  # Resolve relative to repo root (compose mounts use ${MODEL_DIR:-../../../../models-cache})
295	  if [[ "$model_dir" == ../* ]] || [[ "$model_dir" == ./* ]]; then
296	    model_dir="$(cd "${ROOT_DIR:-$(dirname "$0")/..}" && cd "$(dirname "$compose_file")" && cd "$model_dir" 2>/dev/null && pwd)"
297	  fi
298	
299	  local missing=()
300	  local hint_dflash=0
301	  local hint_mtp=0
302	  local hint_gguf=0
303	
304	  # Engine detection: llama.cpp composes mount ${MODEL_DIR}:/models and pass
305	  # `-m /models/<path>`; vLLM composes mount ${MODEL_DIR}:/root/.cache/huggingface
306	  # and pass `--model /root/.cache/huggingface/<subdir>`.
307	  local is_llamacpp=0
308	  if grep -qE 'image:.*ggml-org/llama\.cpp' "$compose_file"; then
309	    is_llamacpp=1
310	  fi
311	
312	  if [[ $is_llamacpp -eq 1 ]]; then
313	    # Scan for `-m /models/<path>` and `--mmproj /models/<path>` to learn what
314	    # the compose actually expects. Falls back to the canonical defaults from
315	    # docker-compose.yml if the variable expansion isn't grep-resolvable.
316	    local gguf_in_container mmproj_in_container
317	    gguf_in_container=$(grep -oE '\-m[[:space:]]+/models/[^[:space:]]+' "$compose_file" \
318	      | head -1 | awk '{print $2}' | sed 's|^/models/||')
319	    mmproj_in_container=$(grep -oE -- '--mmproj[[:space:]]+/models/[^[:space:]]+' "$compose_file" \
320	      | head -1 | awk '{print $2}' | sed 's|^/models/||')
321	
322	    # Strip ${VAR:-default} expansion: take the default after `:-` if present.
323	    gguf_in_container="${gguf_in_container//\$\{GGUF_FILE:-/}"
324	    gguf_in_container="${gguf_in_container%\}}"
325	    mmproj_in_container="${mmproj_in_container//\$\{MMPROJ_FILE:-/}"
326	    mmproj_in_container="${mmproj_in_container%\}}"
327	
328	    [[ -z "$gguf_in_container" ]]   && gguf_in_container="qwen3.6-27b/unsloth-q3kxl/Qwen3.6-27B-UD-Q3_K_XL.gguf"
329	    [[ -z "$mmproj_in_container" ]] && mmproj_in_container="qwen3.6-27b/mmproj-F16.gguf"
330	
331	    if [[ -n "${GGUF_FILE:-}" ]];   then gguf_in_container="$GGUF_FILE";     fi
332	    if [[ -n "${MMPROJ_FILE:-}" ]]; then mmproj_in_container="$MMPROJ_FILE"; fi
333	
334	    if [[ ! -f "${model_dir}/${gguf_in_container}" ]]; then
335	      missing+=("${gguf_in_container} (llama.cpp GGUF weights)")
336	      hint_gguf=1
337	    fi
338	    if [[ ! -f "${model_dir}/${mmproj_in_container}" ]]; then
339	      missing+=("${mmproj_in_container} (vision projector)")
340	      hint_gguf=1
341	    fi
342	  else
343	    # vLLM path — scan for --speculative-config blocks (DFlash draft, MTP head).
344	    # The compose paths reference the in-container path /root/.cache/huggingface/<subdir>;
345	    # the host path is ${MODEL_DIR}/<subdir> (set via the volumes: block).
346	    if grep -qE '"model":[[:space:]]*"/root/.cache/huggingface/qwen3.6-27b-dflash"' "$compose_file"; then
347	      if [[ ! -f "${model_dir}/qwen3.6-27b-dflash/config.json" ]]; then
348	        missing+=("qwen3.6-27b-dflash (DFlash draft model)")
349	        hint_dflash=1
350	      fi
351	    fi
352	    if grep -qE '"model":[[:space:]]*"/root/.cache/huggingface/qwen3.6-27b-mtp-head"' "$compose_file"; then
353	      if [[ ! -f "${model_dir}/qwen3.6-27b-mtp-head/config.json" ]]; then
354	        missing+=("qwen3.6-27b-mtp-head (MTP draft head)")
355	        hint_mtp=1
356	      fi
357	    fi
358	
359	    # vLLM main model — every vLLM compose on this stack mounts AutoRound INT4.
360	    if [[ ! -f "${model_dir}/qwen3.6-27b-autoround-int4/config.json" ]]; then
361	      missing+=("qwen3.6-27b-autoround-int4 (main model)")
362	    fi
363	  fi
364	
365	  if [[ ${#missing[@]} -eq 0 ]]; then
366	    return 0
367	  fi
368	
369	  echo "[preflight] ERROR: compose '$compose_file' expects model files that aren't on host." >&2
370	  for item in "${missing[@]}"; do
371	    echo "[preflight]   missing: ${model_dir}/${item}" >&2
372	  done
373	  echo "[preflight]" >&2
374	  echo "[preflight] Fix:" >&2
375	  if [[ $hint_gguf -eq 1 ]]; then
376	    echo "[preflight]   hf download unsloth/Qwen3.6-27B-GGUF \\" >&2
377	    echo "[preflight]     Qwen3.6-27B-UD-Q3_K_XL.gguf mmproj-F16.gguf \\" >&2
378	    echo "[preflight]     --local-dir ${model_dir}/qwen3.6-27b/unsloth-q3kxl" >&2
379	    echo "[preflight]   # mmproj lands at unsloth-q3kxl/ — move it up so the default --mmproj path resolves:" >&2
380	    echo "[preflight]   #   mv ${model_dir}/qwen3.6-27b/unsloth-q3kxl/mmproj-F16.gguf ${model_dir}/qwen3.6-27b/" >&2
381	    echo "[preflight]   (~16 GB total. setup.sh today only fetches the vLLM AutoRound weights;" >&2
382	    echo "[preflight]    GGUF must be fetched separately for any llamacpp/* variant.)" >&2
383	  fi
384	  if [[ $hint_dflash -eq 1 ]]; then
385	    echo "[preflight]   WITH_DFLASH_DRAFT=1 bash scripts/setup.sh qwen3.6-27b" >&2
386	    echo "[preflight]   (downloads z-lab/Qwen3.6-27B-DFlash, ~1.75 GB; required for dual-dflash* composes)" >&2
387	  fi
388	  if [[ $hint_mtp -eq 1 ]]; then
389	    echo "[preflight]   bash scripts/setup.sh qwen3.6-27b  (re-run with the right flags for MTP head)" >&2
390	  fi
391	  if [[ $hint_dflash -eq 0 ]] && [[ $hint_mtp -eq 0 ]] && [[ $hint_gguf -eq 0 ]]; then
392	    echo "[preflight]   bash scripts/setup.sh qwen3.6-27b" >&2
393	  fi
394	  echo "[preflight] Skip this check:  PREFLIGHT_NO_COMPOSE_DEPS=1 bash scripts/switch.sh ..." >&2
395	  return 1
396	}

398	# preflight_kv_format_hint <compose_file> — soft warning if the target compose
399	# uses a KV format known to be sub-optimal for the user's VRAM class.
400	#
401	# Specifically: dual-turbo.yml uses turboquant_3bit_nc which trips Cliff 2 at 90K
402	# on 20 GB Ampere even on TP=2. See docs/HARDWARE.md + #47 for the cross-rig data.
403	#
404	# Soft warning (returns 0). Skip via: PREFLIGHT_NO_KV_HINT=1
405	preflight_kv_format_hint() {
406	  local compose_file="$1"
407	  if [[ "${PREFLIGHT_NO_KV_HINT:-0}" == "1" ]]; then
408	    return 0
409	  fi
410	  if [[ ! -f "$compose_file" ]] || ! command -v nvidia-smi >/dev/null 2>&1; then
411	    return 0
412	  fi
413	
414	  # Detect smallest VRAM among visible cards (the TP-split ceiling).
415	  local min_vram_mib
416	  min_vram_mib="$(nvidia-smi --query-gpu=memory.total --format=csv,noheader,nounits 2>/dev/null | sort -n | head -1)"
417	  if [[ -z "$min_vram_mib" ]] || [[ "$min_vram_mib" -ge 24000 ]]; then
418	    return 0   # 24 GB+ cards — TQ3 is the right pick, no hint needed
419	  fi
420	
421	  local vram_gb=$((min_vram_mib / 1024))
422	
423	  # Only fire on TQ3-using composes — that's where the 20 GB swap matters.
424	  if grep -qE -- '--kv-cache-dtype[[:space:]]*\n?[[:space:]]*-?[[:space:]]*turboquant_3bit_nc' "$compose_file" 2>/dev/null; then
425	    :
426	  elif grep -qE 'turboquant_3bit_nc' "$compose_file"; then
427	    :
428	  else
429	    return 0   # not a TQ3 compose
430	  fi
431	
432	  echo "[preflight] HINT: smallest GPU has ~${vram_gb} GB VRAM and target compose uses TurboQuant 3-bit KV." >&2
433	  echo "[preflight]       On <24 GB Ampere, TQ3's activation peak during DeltaNet GDN forward exceeds" >&2
434	  echo "[preflight]       the per-card budget after TP split, and Cliff 2 fires at ~90K." >&2
435	  echo "[preflight]       Override with --kv-cache-dtype fp8_e5m2 in the compose file." >&2
436	  echo "[preflight]       Cross-rig validation: docs/HARDWARE.md + club-3090#47" >&2
437	  echo "[preflight]       Predict your config:  bash tools/kv-calc.py --compose <name> --vram ${vram_gb} --kv-format <fp8_e5m2|turboquant_3bit_nc>" >&2
438	  return 0
439	}
440	
441	# autodetect_endpoint — discover the running club-3090 container + its host port.
442	#
443	# Caller-controlled: the bench / verify scripts default URL=http://localhost:8020
444	# and CONTAINER=vllm-qwen36-27b. That assumption breaks when the user is running
445	# a different variant (e.g. dual-turbo on 8011, dual-dflash on 8012, etc.) and
446	# silently makes verify-full / bench / verify-stress emit false negatives because
447	# they're hitting an empty port. Reported by sudepo on club-3090#52.
448	#
449	# Behaviour:
450	#   - If $URL or $CONTAINER is already set in the environment, it WINS — never
451	#     overwritten. This preserves explicit override behaviour.
452	#   - Otherwise, scan `docker ps` for a club-3090-pattern container and extract
453	#     its host port from the port-mapping. Print one [autodetect] line so the
454	#     user knows what we picked.
455	#   - If nothing is detected (no container running, docker unavailable), the
456	#     hardcoded defaults stand — same behaviour as before this helper existed.
457	#
458	# Outputs (mutates env in caller's scope when sourced):
459	#   URL          — http://localhost:<port> if detected
460	#   CONTAINER    — running container name if detected
461	#
462	# Skip via: PREFLIGHT_NO_AUTODETECT=1
463	preflight_autodetect_endpoint() {
464	  if [[ "${PREFLIGHT_NO_AUTODETECT:-0}" == "1" ]]; then
465	    return 0
466	  fi
467	  command -v docker >/dev/null 2>&1 || return 0
468	
469	  local explicit_url="${URL:-}"
470	  local explicit_container="${CONTAINER:-}"
471	  if [[ -n "$explicit_url" && -n "$explicit_container" ]]; then
472	    return 0   # both already set — caller knows what they're doing
473	  fi
474	
475	  # Scan for one of our containers + its `0.0.0.0:<host>->8000/tcp` mapping.
476	  local found_line
477	  found_line=$(docker ps --format '{{.Names}}|{{.Ports}}' 2>/dev/null \
478	    | grep -E '^(vllm-qwen36-27b|llama-cpp-qwen36-27b|vllm-gemma-4-31b)' | head -1)
479	  if [[ -z "$found_line" ]]; then
480	    return 0   # nothing running; defaults stand
481	  fi
482	
483	  local detected_name detected_port
484	  detected_name="${found_line%%|*}"
485	  # Extract host port from "0.0.0.0:8011->8000/tcp", "[::]:8011->8000/tcp",
486	  # or "127.0.0.1:8011->8000/tcp" forms (BIND_HOST=127.0.0.1 produces the last).
487	  # llama-cpp container maps to internal 8080, vllm to 8000 — match both.
488	  detected_port=$(echo "${found_line#*|}" \
489	    | grep -oE '([0-9]{1,3}\.){3}[0-9]{1,3}:[0-9]+->(8000|8080)/tcp' \
490	    | head -1 \
491	    | sed -E 's|^[^:]+:([0-9]+)->.*|\1|')
492	
493	  # Apply, but only fields the user didn't already set explicitly.
494	  if [[ -z "$explicit_container" && -n "$detected_name" ]]; then
495	    CONTAINER="$detected_name"
496	  fi
497	  if [[ -z "$explicit_url" && -n "$detected_port" ]]; then
498	    URL="http://localhost:${detected_port}"
499	  fi
500	
501	  # One-line surface so the user sees what we chose.
502	  if [[ -z "$explicit_url" || -z "$explicit_container" ]]; then
503	    local note=""
504	    [[ -z "$explicit_container" ]] && note="container=${CONTAINER}"
505	    [[ -z "$explicit_url" ]] && note="${note:+$note }url=${URL}"
506	    echo "[autodetect] using running ${note}  (skip: PREFLIGHT_NO_AUTODETECT=1)" >&2
507	  fi
508	  return 0
509	}

1	# ===========================================================================
2	# Bounded-thinking variant — recommended grammar is the DeepSeek scratchpad
3	# (PLAN/NOTE×0-15/VERDICT FSM at `tools/grammar-eval/deepseek-scratchpad.gbnf`).
4	#
5	# **Phase 3 bench 2026-05-04 final (full HE+ 164 + LCB v6 50, n=214):**
6	#   - DeepSeek scratchpad: 93.9% HE+ / 66.0% LCB v6 / 87.4% combined.
7	#     Doesn't lose combined accuracy vs the original andthattoo G/A/E
8	#     grammar (delta = +0.47pp = 1 problem on n=214, well below noise),
9	#     and gains +4pp on LCB v6 (= 2 problems on n=50, design signal not
10	#     statistical proof).
11	#   - andthattoo G/A/E (the originally-published structured-CoT grammar):
12	#     94.5% HE+ / 62.0% LCB / 86.9% combined, tighter think budget
13	#     (mean 134 tokens vs DeepSeek's 541). Slightly better on HE+ alone.
14	#
15	# We ship this single compose with DeepSeek as the recommended grammar
16	# because preserving HE+ accuracy while gaining LCB headroom is the most
17	# defensible per-workload posture — not because of a categorical accuracy
18	# win. The compose itself is
19	# grammar-agnostic — the grammar is selected client-side via
20	# `extra_body={"structured_outputs": {"grammar": ...}}`. Users who want
21	# tighter compression (the ~4× cheaper andthattoo grammar) can pass
22	# `tools/grammar-eval/holiday-tagline.gbnf` (extreme 24-token compression,
23	# wins LCB) or the original andthattoo G/A/E grammar from
24	# https://github.com/andthattoo/structured-cot — see
25	# docs/STRUCTURED_COT.md for the full Phase 3 comparison and grammar
26	# decision table.
27	#
28	# Long-text 214K + structured-CoT grammar enforcement in the reasoning
29	# channel. Compresses think tokens ~30× with iso-or-better accuracy on
30	# coding benchmarks. See docs/STRUCTURED_COT.md for the full bench, the
31	# grammar files, and the harness.
32	#
33	# ⚠️ NOT SAFE FOR IDE-AGENT WORKLOADS WITH TOOL SCHEMAS. Same Cliff 1 mech B
34	# inductor compile-path leak as long-text/long-vision (club-3090#16). Use
35	# `tools-text.yml` for IDE agents until Genesis PN25 lands default-on.
36	#
37	# Additional bug surfaced 2026-05-01 PM on this compose specifically:
38	# `VLLM_SSM_CONV_STATE_LAYOUT=DS` + spec-decode + `mamba_cache_mode='align'`
39	# + `num_accepted_tokens > 1` raises `NotImplementedError` on certain prompt
40	# shapes (LCB/LeetCode-style problems triggered it on every request). Filed
41	# as Sandermage/genesis-vllm-patches#17. Pure long-form text generation with
42	# bounded thinking is unaffected; LCB-style problem statements may crash.
43	#
44	# Stack: identical to long-text (vLLM nightly + Genesis v7.64 with PN12 anchor
45	# sidecar + compile-safe sidecar + PN17 + P104 + P37/P38 + TurboQuant 3-bit KV
46	# + MTP n=3 + 185K text-only at 0.975 mem-util) PLUS one extra flag:
47	# --structured-outputs-config.enable_in_reasoning true. That single flag is
48	# what makes structured-CoT work end-to-end on this stack — vLLM's default
49	# is False, which restricts the grammar mask to post-`</think>` content. Bench
50	# numbers don't reproduce without it (the model thinks freely regardless of
51	# grammar; FREE and FSM produce identical token counts).
52	#
53	# Recommended client max_tokens: 4096. The whole design point of this compose
54	# is bounded thinking — the grammar caps the think block to ~150-300 structured
55	# tokens, then leaves comfortable budget for the answer. 4096 is plenty. (Use
56	# 8192+ if you're calling this compose with FREE thinking, but at that point
57	# you should be on long-text.yml — the structured-outputs flag costs ~10% TPS
58	# for nothing if you're not actually using grammar.)
59	#
60	# When to pick this over long-text:
61	#   - You're using grammar-constrained `<think>` blocks (xgrammar GBNF) to
62	#     bound reasoning cost. See docs/STRUCTURED_COT.md for the harness +
63	#     example grammars (HumanEval+ GOAL/APPROACH/EDGE; LiveCodeBench
64	#     GOAL/STATE/ALGO/EDGE/VERIFY; or your own).
65	#   - You want per-request bounded thinking with structural guarantee.
66	#     PROMPT_TERSE alone (system prompt asking for terse format) often
67	#     suffices on easy problems but loses discipline on hard ones; this
68	#     compose adds the FSM safety net.
69	#   - You're cost-bounded by output token spend and want ~30× cheaper
70	#     thinking with no accuracy regression on coding workloads.
71	#
72	# When NOT to pick this:
73	#   - Anything that doesn't use `structured_outputs.{grammar,json,...}` in
74	#     extra_body — the flag is harmless then but you gain nothing. Use
75	#     long-text instead.
76	#   - Workloads where the model's *unstructured* free thinking is itself
77	#     the product (chat-of-thought UIs, scratch-pad apps). Bounded thinking
78	#     compresses the reasoning into structured slots — fine for code where
79	#     you only consume the answer, not the trace.
80	#   - Vision input — same as long-text, this drops the vision tower to free
81	#     ~1 GB VRAM. Use long-vision-grammar (TODO) if you need both.
82	#
83	# Measured (full bench 2026-04-30 PM, RTX 3090):
84	#   HumanEval+ 164:   FSM 92.7% pass@1, 96 think tok mean, +4.3pp vs FREE,
85	#                     30.7× compression vs FREE thinking (2950 tok mean)
86	#   LiveCodeBench v6: FSM 66.0% pass@1, 145 think tok mean, +24.0pp vs FREE,
87	#                     26.2× compression vs FREE thinking (3797 tok mean)
88	#   Sustained TPS:    ~53 (about 10% slower than long-text due to grammar
89	#                     mask compute; but per-problem wall-clock 10× faster
90	#                     because of the 30× smaller think output)
91	#
92	# Caveat:
93	#   - Some of the +Δpp accuracy gain comes from FSM dodging the
94	#     max_tokens=4096 truncation trap rather than pure reasoning gain.
95	#     If you re-bench at max_tokens=8192 the headline shrinks toward
96	#     andthattoo's published +0.6pp / +14pp. Compression effect is the
97	#     reliably-real win. See docs/STRUCTURED_COT.md "Honest caveats".
98	#   - Grammar × MTP non-determinism: greedy temp=0 runs on different cards
99	#     can produce different verdicts on the same problem (sub-1% rate).
100	#   - Cliff 2 (single-prompt ≥50–60K in GDN) still applies — same as
101	#     long-text. 218K context is for STEADY-STATE accumulation, not
102	#     stuffing 218K of fresh tokens in one request.
103	#   - Cliff 1 mech B leak on real coding-agent workloads (OpenCode, Cline)
104	#     with mixed sys-prompt + tool-schema prefill: same gap as long-text.
105	#     PN12's eager-mode pool is bypassed by inductor-compiled FFN forward.
106	#     See issue #16. Workarounds: switch to `tools-text.yml` (75K + fp8 +
107	#     PN8 reaches compile path), or add `--enforce-eager` below (~20-30%
108	#     TPS hit), or lower `--gpu-memory-utilization` to 0.94.
109	#
110	# Run with:
111	#   cd <repo>/models/qwen3.6-27b/vllm/compose
112	#   docker compose -f docker-compose.bounded-thinking.yml up -d
113	#   # or via switch.sh:
114	#   bash scripts/switch.sh vllm/bounded-thinking
115	#
116	# Then send requests with structured_outputs in extra_body — see
117	# docs/STRUCTURED_COT.md for client examples.
118	# ===========================================================================
119	services:

213	      - GENESIS_ENABLE_PN9_INDEPENDENT_DRAFTER_ATTN=1
214	      - GENESIS_ENABLE_PN11_GDN_AB_CONTIGUOUS=1
215	      - GENESIS_ENABLE_PN12_FFN_INTERMEDIATE_POOL=1
216	      - GENESIS_ENABLE_PN13_CUDA_GRAPH_LAMBDA_ARITY=1
217	      - GENESIS_ENABLE_PN14_TQ_DECODE_OOB_CLAMP=1
218	      - GENESIS_ENABLE_PN17_FA2_LSE_CLAMP=1
219	      - GENESIS_ENABLE_PN25_SILU_INDUCTOR_SAFE=1
220	      # PN30 — RE-ENABLED with our local dst-shaped temp fix
221	      # (`patch_pn30_dst_shaped_temp_fix.py`, applied during setup.sh). The
222	      # original Sander a9977d8 .contiguous() approach corrupted DS row strides
223	      # by raw-memcpying a compact 10240×5 buffer into a 10240×6 destination
224	      # block (row 1+ landed at the wrong destination offset, causing later
225	      # TQ store CUDA assert). Our fix builds a destination-shaped temp inside
226	      # `collect_mamba_copy_meta` and reuses PN30's temp-list lifetime handling.
227	      # Diagnosis credit: ChatGPT/Codex CLI cross-check 2026-05-01 PM.
228	      - GENESIS_ENABLE_PN30_DS_LAYOUT_SPEC_DECODE=1
229	      # PN31 — DISABLED on 1×3090. Per-shape persistent VRAM grows as new
230	      # shapes are seen during prefill; on TP=1 + 24GB, the residence cost
231	      # outpaces the malloc-pressure relief. Sander explicitly warned in
232	      # `753344b` he couldn't validate on 24GB; cross-rig validated 2026-05-01
233	      # PM here that 0.95→0.93 mem-util can't carry both PN31 + DeltaNet
234	      # `chunk_fwd_o` activation peak at 29K depth. Reported back on
235	      # Sandermage/genesis-vllm-patches#15. Use tools-text.yml fp8 path for
236	      # 25K+ tool-RETURN workloads in the meantime.
237	      # - GENESIS_ENABLE_PN31_FA_VARLEN_PERSISTENT_OUT=1
238	      - GENESIS_ENABLE_PN19_SCOPED_MAX_SPLIT=1
239	      - GENESIS_ENABLE_PN22_LOCAL_ARGMAX_TP=1
240	      # PN26b sparse-V Triton kernel — first SM86 (Ampere consumer) sparse-V
241	      # kernel in any public tree. 27B-specific tuning per Sandermage's PROD.
242	      - GENESIS_ENABLE_PN26_SPARSE_V=1
243	      - GENESIS_ENABLE_PN59_STREAMING_GDN=1
244	      - GENESIS_PN59_DEBUG=1
245	      - GENESIS_PN26_SPARSE_V_BLOCK_KV=8
246	      - GENESIS_PN26_SPARSE_V_NUM_WARPS=4
247	      - GENESIS_PN26_SPARSE_V_THRESHOLD=0.01
248	      # v7.65 #14 + #15 fixes (compile-safe in-source hook + FA varlen clamp).
249	      # Replace our local patch_pn12_compile_safe_custom_op.py + P104 sidecars.
250	      - GENESIS_ENABLE_P38B_COMPILE_SAFE=1
251	      - GENESIS_ENABLE_P15B_FA_VARLEN_CLAMP=1
252	      - GENESIS_PREALLOC_TOKEN_BUDGET=4128
253	      - GENESIS_BUFFER_MODE=shared
254	      # Explicit OFFs to match Sandermage's PROD env-var set verbatim:
255	      # P78 (P78_TOLIST_CAPTURE_GUARD) — superseded by our patch_tolist_cudagraph.py
256	      # P81 (FP8 block-scaled M<=8) — FP8-specific, no-op on our TQ3 path
257	      # P82 — biased on small-batch single-stream Lorbus INT4 + MTP K=3 (Sander PROD)
258	      - GENESIS_ENABLE_P78_TOLIST_CAPTURE_GUARD=0
259	      - GENESIS_ENABLE_P81_FP8_BLOCK_SCALED_M_LE_8=0
260	      # P82 — off by default (biased toward Lorbus INT4 + MTP K=3 single-stream).
261	      # Enable via .env: GENESIS_ENABLE_P82=1 to test on this rig.
262	      - GENESIS_ENABLE_P82=${GENESIS_ENABLE_P82:-0}
263	      - GENESIS_P82_THRESHOLD_SINGLE=0.3
264	      # P40 — TQ k8v4 GQA grouping kernel (+15-30% on compute-regime GPUs with L2>=24MB).
265	      # RTX 5090 Laptop: 88MB L2 → enable via .env: GENESIS_ENABLE_P40=1
266	      # RTX 3090: 6MB L2 → no gain, default off.
267	      - GENESIS_ENABLE_P40=${GENESIS_ENABLE_P40:-0}
268	      # vLLM env knobs from Sandermage's launch:
269	      # VLLM_SSM_CONV_STATE_LAYOUT=DS — RE-ENABLED with our local PN30 fix
270	      # (`patch_pn30_dst_shaped_temp_fix.py`, applied during setup.sh).
271	      # Without our fix, Sander's PN30 a9977d8 corrupts DS row strides on
272	      # spec-decode AL>1 paths. With our fix, PN30 builds a destination-shaped
273	      # temp instead of a compact one, preserving DS layout. +6% TPS retained.
274	      - VLLM_SSM_CONV_STATE_LAYOUT=DS
275	      - VLLM_USE_FUSED_MOE_GROUPED_TOPK=1
276	    shm_size: "16gb"
277	    ipc: host
278	    deploy:
279	      resources:
280	        reservations:
281	          devices:
282	            - driver: nvidia
283	              count: all
284	              capabilities: [gpu]
285	    entrypoint:
286	      - /bin/bash
287	      - -c
288	      - |
289	        set -e
290	        pip install xxhash pandas scipy -q
291	        # Pre-flight: Genesis patches must be populated. Empty volume mount
292	        # = silent no-op apply_all = boot fails later with cryptic upstream
293	        # error (e.g. "TurboQuant KV not supported for hybrid models", #13).
294	        if [ ! -f /usr/local/lib/python3.12/dist-packages/vllm/_genesis/patches/apply_all.py ]; then
295	          echo "ERROR: Genesis patches missing — host volume models/qwen3.6-27b/vllm/patches/genesis/ is empty." >&2
296	          echo "       Run from repo root before 'docker compose up':" >&2
297	          echo "         bash scripts/setup.sh qwen3.6-27b" >&2
298	          exit 1
299	        fi
300	        python3 -m vllm._genesis.patches.apply_all
301	        # Tool-parser deferred-commit fix for qwen3coder SSE-silence bug (issue #72).
302	        # Drops out when vllm-project/vllm lands the upstream fix.
303	        python3 /patches/qwen3coder_tool_parser_deferred_commit.py
304	        # patch_tolist_cudagraph.py — still needed pending upstream
305	        # vllm#40807 .tolist() capture-stream guard fix.
306	        # patch_inputs_embeds_optional.py — backport of vllm#35975
307	        # (free ~64 MiB GPU + ~64 MiB pinned CPU on text-only models).
308	        # Drops when PR merges upstream.
309	        # VLLM_ENFORCE_EAGER=1 in compose/.env disables CUDA graphs — use on
310	        # hardware where Cliff 2 GDN activation spikes occur at runtime.
311	        exec vllm serve ${VLLM_ENFORCE_EAGER:+--enforce-eager} "$@"
312	      - --
313	    command:
314	      - --model
315	      - /root/.cache/huggingface/qwen3.6-27b-autoround-int4
316	      - --served-model-name
317	      - qwen3.6-27b-autoround
318	      - --quantization
319	      - auto_round
320	      - --dtype
321	      - float16
322	      - --tensor-parallel-size
323	      - "1"
324	      # 180K + 0.97 — backed off from 214K + 0.985 on 2026-05-01 PM after
325	      # verify-stress probe 1 (10K-token long-context needle) crashed with
326	      # GDN forward OOM (`fla.ops.chunk.chunk_gated_delta_rule_fwd_h`
327	      # `k.new_empty(B, NT, H, V, K)` requested 98 MiB, 46 MiB free). At
328	      # 0.985 mem-util, activation budget was ~46 MiB at 10K prefill —
329	      # tight enough that ANY single prompt over a few thousand tokens was
330	      # a coin flip. The "Cliff 2 fires only at 50-60K" framing was
331	      # config-specific to the dev205 + 0.92 default; at tighter mem-util
332	      # the GDN forward buffer outpaces available activation much earlier.
333	      #
334	      # New target (validated 2026-05-01 PM via verify-stress.sh):
335	      # 180K + 0.97 → ~6.4 GB after weights, ~5.9 GB KV pool, ~500 MiB
336	      # activation safety. Probe 1 (10K needle) and probe 6 (math reasoning
337	      # at max_tokens=8192) PASS. Safe single-prompt envelope ~30K; full
338	      # 180K context for steady-state turn accumulation only.
339	      #
340	      # IDE-agent workloads (sys + tool schemas) STILL crash here per
341	      # club-3090#16 — different bug (Cliff 1 mech B inductor leak), not
342	      # fixed by mem-util tuning. Use tools-text.yml for those.
343	      - --max-model-len
344	      - "${MAX_MODEL_LEN:-180000}"
345	      # 0.95 — backed off from 0.97 on 2026-05-01 PM after PN25 v3 closed
346	      # Cliff 1 mech B at the FFN intermediate buffer (which now sits in
347	      # PN12+PN25's resident FFNIntermediateCache pool). Pool residence
348	      # adds ~140 MiB persistent VRAM, which tightened activation budget
349	      # at DeltaNet `chunk_fwd_o` (`o = torch.empty_like(v)`, 50 MiB) —
350	      # 0.97 left only 26 MiB free, OOM at 9.8K probe. 0.95 frees ~480 MiB
351	      # activation budget, validated up to 30K probes.
352	      - --gpu-memory-utilization
353	      - "${GPU_MEMORY_UTILIZATION:-0.93}"
354	      - --max-num-seqs
355	      - "1"
356	      - --max-num-batched-tokens
357	      - "${MAX_NUM_BATCHED_TOKENS:-4128}"
358	      - --kv-cache-dtype
359	      - turboquant_3bit_nc
360	      # Drops the vision tower → frees ~1 GB VRAM → lifts engine ceiling from
361	      # 198K (long-vision at 0.98) to 218K (this variant at 0.985). This is the only difference vs long-vision.
362	      - --language-model-only
363	      - --trust-remote-code
364	      - --reasoning-parser
365	      - qwen3
366	      - --enable-auto-tool-choice
367	      - --tool-call-parser
368	      - qwen3_coder
369	      - --enable-prefix-caching
370	      - --enable-chunked-prefill
371	      - --no-scheduler-reserve-full-isl
372	      - --speculative-config
373	      - '{"method":"mtp","num_speculative_tokens":3}'
374	      - --host
375	      - 0.0.0.0
376	      - --port
377	      - "8000"

user_msg: Read <repo>/scripts/soak-test.sh and tell me what's relevant to the issue.
tool_result:
1	#!/usr/bin/env bash
2	#
3	# Soak test - runtime VRAM accretion / multi-turn traffic validation.
4	#
5	# Run before shipping config, Genesis, vLLM, or memory-policy changes that can
6	# pass verify-full.sh and verify-stress.sh but still accrete VRAM under
7	# repeated agent turns. This is intentionally slow and not part of launch.
8	#
9	# Scope:
10	#   - Single-stream multi-turn agent traffic, no concurrency stress.
11	#   - Watches VRAM growth, engine liveness, TTFT growth, and decode TPS
12	#     retention across sessions.
13	#   - Read-only against the running deployment.
14	#
15	# Time budget:
16	#   Default SOAK_SESSIONS=20 x SOAK_TURNS=5, capped by SOAK_TIMEOUT_S=1800.
17	#   Expect 10-30 minutes depending on config.
18	#
19	# Usage:
20	#   CONTAINER=vllm-qwen36-27b-long-vision bash scripts/soak-test.sh
21	#   ENDPOINT=http://localhost:8020 SOAK_SESSIONS=8 bash scripts/soak-test.sh
22	#
23	# Env:
24	#   CONTAINER              Running container. Default: first vllm-qwen36-27b*
25	#                          container from `docker ps`.
26	#   ENDPOINT / URL         OpenAI endpoint. Default: mapped container port for
27	#                          8000/tcp, falling back to http://localhost:8020.
28	#   MODEL                  Served model. Default: first id from /v1/models.
29	#   SOAK_MODE              "fresh" (default) — each turn is an independent
30	#                          conversation; tests raw VRAM accretion across
31	#                          requests. "continuous" — each session is one
32	#                          multi-turn agentic conversation that ramps to
33	#                          ~22-25K accumulated context by turn 5; tests the
34	#                          context-accumulation accretion class that bit
35	#                          club-3090#41 (hermes/openhands traffic).
36	#   SOAK_SESSIONS          Independent sessions. Default: 20.
37	#   SOAK_TURNS             Turns per session, max 5 fixture shapes. Default: 5.
38	#                          (Continuous mode requires SOAK_TURNS=5 — the
39	#                          turn shapes are designed to ramp; partial
40	#                          sessions don't reach the target context size.)
41	#   SOAK_MAX_GROWTH_MIB    Fail if max VRAM growth exceeds this after warm
42	#                          baseline. Default: 200 MiB.
43	#   SOAK_TIMEOUT_S         Hard wall-clock cap. Default: 1800 seconds.
44	#   SOAK_REQ_TIMEOUT_S     Per-request timeout. Default: 600 seconds.
45	#   SOAK_OUTPUT            Output dir. Default: results/soak-YYYYmmdd-HHMMSS.
46	#
47	# Outputs:
48	#   results/<run>/baseline.json
49	#   results/<run>/turn-log.csv
50	#   results/<run>/gpu-log.csv
51	#   results/<run>/summary.md
52	#
53	# Exit codes:
54	#   0 pass
55	#   1 fail
56	#   2 inconclusive / timeout / preflight could not run
57	
58	set -euo pipefail
59	
60	SOAK_SESSIONS="${SOAK_SESSIONS:-20}"
61	SOAK_TURNS="${SOAK_TURNS:-5}"
62	SOAK_MODE="${SOAK_MODE:-fresh}"
63	SOAK_MAX_GROWTH_MIB="${SOAK_MAX_GROWTH_MIB:-200}"
64	SOAK_TIMEOUT_S="${SOAK_TIMEOUT_S:-1800}"
65	SOAK_REQ_TIMEOUT_S="${SOAK_REQ_TIMEOUT_S:-600}"
66	SOAK_OUTPUT="${SOAK_OUTPUT:-results/soak-$(date +%Y%m%d-%H%M%S)}"
67	
68	case "$SOAK_MODE" in
69	  fresh|continuous) ;;
70	  *) echo "ERROR: SOAK_MODE='${SOAK_MODE}' — must be 'fresh' or 'continuous'." >&2; exit 2 ;;
71	esac
72	if [[ "$SOAK_MODE" == "continuous" && "$SOAK_TURNS" -ne 5 ]]; then
73	  echo "ERROR: continuous mode requires SOAK_TURNS=5 (got ${SOAK_TURNS}). Turn shapes are designed to ramp; partial runs don't reach target context size." >&2
74	  exit 2
75	fi
76	
77	REPO_ROOT="$(cd "$(dirname "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}")/.." && pwd)"
78	HELPER="${REPO_ROOT}/scripts/soak-helper.py"
79	cd "$REPO_ROOT"
80	
81	log() { printf '[soak] %s\n' "$*"; }
82	die() { log "ERROR: $*"; exit 2; }
83	need() { command -v "$1" >/dev/null 2>&1 || die "'$1' not found in PATH"; }
84	soft_need() { command -v "$1" >/dev/null 2>&1; }
85	
86	need curl
87	need nvidia-smi
88	need python3
89	[[ -x "$HELPER" || -f "$HELPER" ]] || die "missing helper: $HELPER"
90	
91	# docker is soft-required: only needed for container-mode tracking
92	# (docker stats + docker logs scrape). Host engines (e.g. llama.cpp host
93	# build, see #85, #87) use CONTAINER=none and run without docker.
94	HAVE_DOCKER=0
95	if soft_need docker; then HAVE_DOCKER=1; fi
96	if [[ "${CONTAINER:-}" == "none" ]]; then
97	  HOST_MODE=1
98	elif [[ "$HAVE_DOCKER" == "0" ]]; then
99	  log "docker not in PATH — running in host mode (CONTAINER=none implied)"
100	  HOST_MODE=1
101	  CONTAINER="none"
102	else
103	  HOST_MODE=0
104	fi
105	
106	auto_container() {
107	  docker ps --format '{{.Names}}' 2>/dev/null \
108	    | grep -E '^(vllm-qwen36-27b|vllm-gemma-4-31b)' \
109	    | head -1 || true
110	}
111	
112	endpoint_from_container() {
113	  local container="$1"
114	  local mapped port
115	  mapped="$(docker port "$container" 8000/tcp 2>/dev/null | head -1 || true)"
116	  if [[ -n "$mapped" ]]; then
117	    port="${mapped##*:}"
118	    [[ "$port" =~ ^[0-9]+$ ]] && { printf 'http://localhost:%s\n' "$port"; return 0; }
119	  fi
120	  printf 'http://localhost:8020\n'
121	}
122	
123	vram_mib() {
124	  nvidia-smi --query-gpu=memory.used --format=csv,noheader,nounits 2>/dev/null \
125	    | awk -F, '{gsub(/ /, "", $1); sum += $1} END {print sum + 0}'
126	}
127	
128	append_gpu_snapshot() {
129	  local session="$1"
130	  local turn="$2"
131	  nvidia-smi --query-gpu=index,memory.used,utilization.gpu --format=csv,noheader,nounits 2>/dev/null \
132	    | awk -F, -v s="$session" -v t="$turn" '{
133	        for (i = 1; i <= NF; i++) gsub(/^ +| +$/, "", $i)
134	        printf "%s,%s,%s,%s,%s\n", s, t, $1, $2, $3
135	      }' >> "$GPU_LOG" || true
136	}
137	
138	capture_state() {
139	  local label="$1"
140	  nvidia-smi --query-gpu=index,name,memory.used,memory.total,utilization.gpu,power.draw,temperature.gpu \
141	    --format=csv,noheader,nounits > "${SOAK_OUTPUT}/nvidia-smi-${label}.csv" 2>/dev/null || true
142	  if [[ "$HOST_MODE" == "0" ]]; then
143	    docker stats --no-stream --format '{{json .}}' "$CONTAINER" \
144	      > "${SOAK_OUTPUT}/docker-stats-${label}.jsonl" 2>/dev/null || true
145	  fi
146	}
147	
148	finish() {
149	  local rc=$?
150	  capture_state "final"
151	  log "artifacts: ${SOAK_OUTPUT}"
152	  exit "$rc"
153	}
154	trap finish EXIT
155	trap 'log "interrupted"; exit 2' INT TERM
156	
157	if [[ "$HOST_MODE" == "1" ]]; then
158	  log "host mode: CONTAINER=none — skipping docker checks (URL must be set or auto-detected)"
159	  CONTAINER="none"
160	else
161	  CONTAINER="${CONTAINER:-$(auto_container)}"
162	  [[ -n "$CONTAINER" ]] || die "no running vllm-qwen36-27b*/vllm-gemma-4-31b* container found; set CONTAINER=... or CONTAINER=none for host engines"
163	  docker inspect "$CONTAINER" >/dev/null 2>&1 || die "container '$CONTAINER' not found (use CONTAINER=none for host engine builds)"
164	  [[ "$(docker inspect -f '{{.State.Running}}' "$CONTAINER" 2>/dev/null || echo false)" == "true" ]] \
165	    || die "container '$CONTAINER' is not running"
166	fi
167	
168	if [[ "$HOST_MODE" == "1" ]]; then
169	  # Host mode: URL must be set explicitly (or fall back to localhost:8020).
170	  # We can't sniff a port from a container that doesn't exist.
171	  ENDPOINT="${ENDPOINT:-${URL:-http://localhost:8020}}"
172	else
173	  ENDPOINT="${ENDPOINT:-${URL:-$(endpoint_from_container "$CONTAINER")}}"
174	fi
175	mkdir -p "$SOAK_OUTPUT"
176	
177	MODELS_JSON="${SOAK_OUTPUT}/models.json"
178	curl -sf -m 10 "${ENDPOINT}/v1/models" -o "$MODELS_JSON" \
179	  || die "no response from ${ENDPOINT}/v1/models"
180	MODEL="${MODEL:-$(python3 "$HELPER" model "$MODELS_JSON")}"
181	
182	TURN_LOG="${SOAK_OUTPUT}/turn-log.csv"
183	GPU_LOG="${SOAK_OUTPUT}/gpu-log.csv"
184	SUMMARY_MD="${SOAK_OUTPUT}/summary.md"
185	REQUEST_DIR="${SOAK_OUTPUT}/requests"
186	RESPONSE_DIR="${SOAK_OUTPUT}/responses"
187	STATE_DIR="${SOAK_OUTPUT}/states"
188	mkdir -p "$REQUEST_DIR" "$RESPONSE_DIR" "$STATE_DIR"
189	
190	printf 'session_id,turn_id,t_ms,vram_mib,ttft_ms,decode_tps,completion_tokens,status,error\n' > "$TURN_LOG"
191	printf 'session_id,turn_id,gpu_index,memory_used_mib,utilization_gpu_pct\n' > "$GPU_LOG"
192	
193	capture_state "baseline"
194	python3 "$HELPER" baseline "$SOAK_OUTPUT" "$CONTAINER" "$ENDPOINT" "$MODEL" \
195	  "$SOAK_SESSIONS" "$SOAK_TURNS" "$SOAK_MAX_GROWTH_MIB"
196	
197	log "running soak test against ${ENDPOINT} (model=${MODEL}, container=${CONTAINER})"
198	log "mode=${SOAK_MODE} sessions=${SOAK_SESSIONS} turns=${SOAK_TURNS} max_growth=${SOAK_MAX_GROWTH_MIB}MiB timeout=${SOAK_TIMEOUT_S}s"
199	log "output=${SOAK_OUTPUT}"
200	
201	START_SECONDS="$SECONDS"
202	BOOT_VRAM_MIB=""
203	TIMED_OUT=0
204	
205	for session in $(seq 1 "$SOAK_SESSIONS"); do
206	  log "session ${session}/${SOAK_SESSIONS}"
207	  state_file="${STATE_DIR}/state-s${session}.json"
208	  if [[ "$SOAK_MODE" == "continuous" ]]; then
209	    python3 "$HELPER" init-session "$state_file" "$session"
210	  fi
211	  for turn in $(seq 1 "$SOAK_TURNS"); do
212	    if (( SECONDS - START_SECONDS >= SOAK_TIMEOUT_S )); then
213	      TIMED_OUT=1
214	      log "timeout reached before session=${session} turn=${turn}"
215	      break 2
216	    fi
217	
218	    req_file="${REQUEST_DIR}/s${session}-t${turn}.json"
219	    metrics_file="${RESPONSE_DIR}/s${session}-t${turn}.metrics.json"
220	    if [[ "$SOAK_MODE" == "continuous" ]]; then
221	      python3 "$HELPER" request-continuous "$MODEL" "$state_file" "$turn" "$req_file"
222	    else
223	      python3 "$HELPER" request "$MODEL" "$session" "$turn" "$req_file"
224	    fi
225	    python3 "$HELPER" run "$ENDPOINT" "$req_file" "$SOAK_REQ_TIMEOUT_S" "$metrics_file"
226	    if [[ "$SOAK_MODE" == "continuous" ]]; then
227	      python3 "$HELPER" ingest "$state_file" "$metrics_file" "$turn"
228	    fi
229	
230	    vram="$(vram_mib)"
231	    append_gpu_snapshot "$session" "$turn"
232	    python3 "$HELPER" append-log "$TURN_LOG" "$session" "$turn" "$vram" "$metrics_file"
233	
234	    read -r status t_ms ttft_ms decode_tps < <(python3 "$HELPER" metric "$metrics_file")
235	    log "  turn ${turn}/${SOAK_TURNS}: status=${status} wall=${t_ms}ms ttft=${ttft_ms}ms decode_tps=${decode_tps} vram=${vram}MiB"
236	  done
237	
238	  # Capture warm baseline at END of session 1 — after all 5 turn shapes have
239	  # run once and prefix cache has filled. Real accretion is measured FROM
240	  # this baseline across sessions 2-N, so cache-fill (typically +500-1500
241	  # MiB on the first 12K-char tool-result paste) doesn't false-positive.
242	  # Calibration validated 2026-05-03 on long-text @ 0.93 + 180K — sessions
243	  # 2-10 stayed flat at session-1-end VRAM, confirming the test discriminates
244	  # cache fill from accretion correctly.
245	  if [[ -z "$BOOT_VRAM_MIB" ]]; then
246	    BOOT_VRAM_MIB="$(vram_mib)"
247	    log "warm baseline after session 1: ${BOOT_VRAM_MIB} MiB"
248	  fi
249	done
250	
251	if [[ -z "$BOOT_VRAM_MIB" ]]; then
252	  BOOT_VRAM_MIB="$(vram_mib)"
253	  TIMED_OUT=1
254	  log "no completed turns; writing inconclusive summary"
255	fi
256	
257	set +e
258	python3 "$HELPER" summary "$TURN_LOG" "$SUMMARY_MD" "$BOOT_VRAM_MIB" \
259	  "$SOAK_MAX_GROWTH_MIB" "$TIMED_OUT" "$SOAK_SESSIONS"
260	rc=$?
261	set -e
262	exit "$rc"
263

1	#!/usr/bin/env bash
2	# scripts/report.sh — paste-ready triage report for club-3090
3	#
4	# Run when filing a bug report, sharing cross-rig benchmark data, or replying
5	# to a triage thread. Captures hardware, OS, GPU, container runtime, stack
6	# version, and active container state in markdown ready to paste into a GitHub
7	# issue or discussion.
8	#
9	# Usage:
10	#   bash scripts/report.sh                   # default: hardware + stack + boot log highlights (~2 sec)
11	#   bash scripts/report.sh --verify          # adds verify-full.sh output (~1-2 min)
12	#   bash scripts/report.sh --stress          # adds verify-stress.sh 7/7 output (~5-10 min)
13	#   bash scripts/report.sh --soak            # adds SOAK_MODE=continuous summary (~25 min) — catches Cliff 2b
14	#   bash scripts/report.sh --bench           # adds bench.sh output (~3 min)
15	#   bash scripts/report.sh --agentic         # adds bench-agentic.sh output (1 session × 12 turns, ~8 min)
16	#   bash scripts/report.sh --full            # ALL five: verify + stress + soak + bench + agentic (~43 min, the canonical "everything" pass for cross-rig contributions)
17	#   bash scripts/report.sh --no-redact       # disable path/host/user redaction
18	#   bash scripts/report.sh --container NAME  # override container auto-detection
19	#   bash scripts/report.sh > my-rig.md       # capture for paste
20	#
21	# Why --soak is its own flag:
22	#   verify-full + verify-stress + bench all PASS on configs that FAIL the
23	#   multi-turn continuous soak (Cliff 2b at ~25K accumulated tokens). Until
24	#   the upstream fix lands, soak is the only test that catches the agentic-
25	#   workload failure mode. See docs/CLIFFS.md.
26	#
27	# By default, paths under user homes, hostnames, usernames, and HF tokens are
28	# redacted. Use --no-redact for internal sharing only.
29	
30	set -uo pipefail
31	
32	DO_VERIFY=0
33	DO_STRESS=0
34	DO_SOAK=0
35	DO_BENCH=0
36	DO_AGENTIC=0
37	REDACT=1
38	CONTAINER=""
39	
40	print_help() {
41	  sed -n '2,/^set/p' "$0" | sed 's/^# \?//' | head -n -1
42	}
43	
44	while [[ $# -gt 0 ]]; do
45	  case "$1" in
46	    --verify) DO_VERIFY=1; shift ;;
47	    --stress) DO_STRESS=1; shift ;;
48	    --soak) DO_SOAK=1; shift ;;
49	    --bench) DO_BENCH=1; shift ;;
50	    --agentic) DO_AGENTIC=1; shift ;;
51	    --full) DO_VERIFY=1; DO_STRESS=1; DO_SOAK=1; DO_BENCH=1; DO_AGENTIC=1; shift ;;
52	    --no-redact) REDACT=0; shift ;;
53	    --container) CONTAINER="${2:-}"; shift 2 ;;
54	    -h|--help) print_help; exit 0 ;;
55	    *) echo "Unknown arg: $1" >&2; echo "Try: bash scripts/report.sh --help" >&2; exit 1 ;;
56	  esac
57	done

64	cd "$REPO_ROOT"

71	    sed \
72	      -e "s|/home/${USER_NAME}|~|g" \
73	      -e "s|/root|~|g" \
74	      -e "s|${HOST_SHORT}|<HOST>|g" \
75	      -e "s|${USER_NAME}|<USER>|g" \
76	      -e 's|HF_TOKEN=[^ "]*|HF_TOKEN=<REDACTED>|g' \
77	      -e 's|HUGGING_FACE_HUB_TOKEN=[^ "]*|HUGGING_FACE_HUB_TOKEN=<REDACTED>|g' \
78	      -e 's|api_key=[^ "]*|api_key=<REDACTED>|gi' \
79	      -e 's|hf_[A-Za-z0-9]\{30,\}|hf_<REDACTED>|g'
80	  else
81	    cat
82	  fi
83	}

91	  cat
92	  printf '```\n\n</details>\n'
93	}

153	    cpu_cores=$(lscpu 2>/dev/null | awk -F: '/^CPU\(s\):/ {gsub(/ /, "", $2); print $2; exit}')
154	    echo "- **CPU:** ${cpu_model:-unknown} (${cpu_cores:-?} threads)"

157	  fi

161	    ram_avail=$(free -h 2>/dev/null | awk '/^Mem:/ {print $7}')
162	    echo "- **RAM:** ${ram_total} total, ${ram_avail} available"
163	    swap_total=$(free -h 2>/dev/null | awk '/^Swap:/ {print $2}')

172	section "Disk"
173	{
174	  declare -a checked_paths=()
175	  add_disk_row() {
176	    local p="$1"
177	    [[ -z "$p" || ! -d "$p" ]] && return
178	    for seen in "${checked_paths[@]:-}"; do
179	      [[ "$seen" == "$p" ]] && return
180	    done
181	    checked_paths+=("$p")
182	    local fs avail
183	    fs=$(df -T "$p" 2>/dev/null | awk 'NR==2 {print $2}')
184	    avail=$(df -h "$p" 2>/dev/null | awk 'NR==2 {print $4}')

198	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
199	# GPU hardware
200	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
201	
202	section "GPU hardware"
203	if ! have nvidia-smi; then
204	  echo "_nvidia-smi not available — no NVIDIA GPU detected or driver not installed_"
205	else
206	  {
207	    nvidia-smi --query-gpu=index,name,memory.total,driver_version,vbios_version,persistence_mode,power.limit,power.default_limit,power.max_limit,power.draw,pci.bus_id,pcie.link.gen.current,pcie.link.gen.max,pcie.link.width.current,pcie.link.width.max \
208	      --format=csv,noheader 2>/dev/null \
209	      | while IFS=, read -r idx name memtotal driver vbios persistence pwr_limit pwr_default pwr_max pwr_draw bus_id pcie_gen_cur pcie_gen_max pcie_width_cur pcie_width_max; do
210	          # trim leading spaces from CSV fields
211	          idx="${idx# }"; name="${name# }"; memtotal="${memtotal# }"
212	          driver="${driver# }"; vbios="${vbios# }"; persistence="${persistence# }"
213	          pwr_limit="${pwr_limit# }"; pwr_default="${pwr_default# }"
214	          pwr_max="${pwr_max# }"; pwr_draw="${pwr_draw# }"
215	          bus_id="${bus_id# }"; pcie_gen_cur="${pcie_gen_cur# }"; pcie_gen_max="${pcie_gen_max# }"
216	          pcie_width_cur="${pcie_width_cur# }"; pcie_width_max="${pcie_width_max# }"
217	
218	          # Flag if user has capped below default
219	          power_note=""
220	          pwr_limit_w="${pwr_limit% W}"; pwr_limit_w="${pwr_limit_w%.*}"
221	          pwr_default_w="${pwr_default% W}"; pwr_default_w="${pwr_default_w%.*}"
222	          if [[ "$pwr_limit_w" =~ ^[0-9]+$ ]] && [[ "$pwr_default_w" =~ ^[0-9]+$ ]]; then
223	            if [[ "$pwr_limit_w" -lt "$pwr_default_w" ]]; then
224	              power_note=" ⚠ user-capped below default"
225	            elif [[ "$pwr_limit_w" -gt "$pwr_default_w" ]]; then
226	              power_note=" (overclocked above default)"
227	            fi
228	          fi
229	
230	          # Flag if PCIe lane width is below max — that's hardware-level (slot
231	          # has fewer lanes wired, riser cables, BIOS bifurcation, etc.) and
232	          # affects model load speed + per-card all-reduce bandwidth.
233	          # NOTE: pcie.link.gen.current drops to Gen 1 at idle for power
234	          # saving — that's normal, not a degradation. Re-check under load if
235	          # you want the actual negotiated gen. Width is hardware-fixed.
236	          pcie_note=""
237	          if [[ -n "$pcie_width_cur" && -n "$pcie_width_max" && "$pcie_width_cur" != "$pcie_width_max" ]]; then
238	            pcie_note=" ⚠ slot is narrower than GPU capability — affects load + all-reduce bandwidth"
239	          fi
240	
241	          echo "- **GPU $idx:** $name | $memtotal | driver $driver | VBIOS $vbios | persistence=$persistence"
242	          echo "  - **Power:** limit=${pwr_limit} (default=${pwr_default}, max=${pwr_max}) | current_draw=${pwr_draw}${power_note}"
243	          echo "  - **PCIe:** x${pcie_width_cur} lanes negotiated (GPU max x${pcie_width_max}, Gen up to ${pcie_gen_max}) | bus ${bus_id}${pcie_note}"
244	        done
245	
246	    cuda_ver=$(nvidia-smi 2>/dev/null | grep -oE 'CUDA Version: [0-9.]+' | head -1 | awk '{print $3}')
247	    [[ -n "$cuda_ver" ]] && echo "- **CUDA Runtime (per driver):** $cuda_ver"
248	
249	    # Persistence mode + ECC summary
250	    ecc_status=$(nvidia-smi --query-gpu=ecc.mode.current --format=csv,noheader 2>/dev/null | head -1 | tr -d ' ')
251	    [[ -n "$ecc_status" ]] && echo "- **ECC mode:** $ecc_status (3090s don't have ECC; expect N/A)"
252	  } | redact
253	
254	  subsection "NVLink"
255	  if nvidia-smi nvlink --status -i 0 2>/dev/null | grep -qE 'Link [0-9]+:'; then
256	    nvidia-smi nvlink --status 2>&1 | redact | details "NVLink link status"
257	  else
258	    echo "_No NVLink detected (PCIe-only)_"
259	  fi
260	
261	  subsection "Topology"
262	  nvidia-smi topo -m 2>&1 | redact | details "PCIe / GPU topology matrix"
263	
264	  subsection "Full nvidia-smi"
265	  nvidia-smi 2>&1 | redact | details "Full nvidia-smi output"
266	fi

343	
344	section "Stack version"
345	{
346	  if [[ -d .git ]]; then
347	    commit=$(git rev-parse --short HEAD 2>/dev/null)
348	    branch=$(git branch --show-current 2>/dev/null)
349	    echo "- **club-3090:** \`${commit:-unknown}\` (branch: \`${branch:-detached}\`)"
350	    if ! git diff --quiet 2>/dev/null || ! git diff --cached --quiet 2>/dev/null; then
351	      echo "- **Working tree:** ⚠ has uncommitted changes (run \`git status\` to inspect)"
352	    fi
353	  else
354	    echo "- **club-3090:** not a git repo"
355	  fi
356	
357	  if [[ -f scripts/setup.sh ]]; then
358	    # Parse `GENESIS_PIN="${GENESIS_PIN:-<default>}"` — extract just the default value
359	    genesis_pin=$(grep -E '^GENESIS_PIN=' scripts/setup.sh 2>/dev/null | head -1 \
360	      | sed -E 's/.*:-([^}]+)\}.*/\1/; t; s/.*=//' \
361	      | tr -d '"' | tr -d "'")
362	    [[ -n "$genesis_pin" ]] && echo "- **GENESIS_PIN default:** \`$genesis_pin\` (per scripts/setup.sh)"
363	    # Override from env if set
364	    [[ -n "${GENESIS_PIN:-}" ]] && echo "- **GENESIS_PIN env override:** \`$GENESIS_PIN\`"

369	    if [[ -n "$cached" ]]; then
370	      echo "- **Cached vLLM images:**"
371	      echo "$cached" | while read -r tag digest age rest; do
372	        echo "  - tag \`$tag\` digest \`$digest\` ($age $rest)"
373	      done
374	    fi
375	  fi

378	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
379	# Active container
380	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
381	
382	section "Active container"
383	# Engine-agnostic auto-detection: try vllm-* first (most common on this stack),
384	# fall back to llama-cpp-* (the alternate engine we ship). User can override
385	# with CONTAINER=... env var for non-standard naming (microk8s deployments,
386	# host engine builds via CONTAINER=none, etc.).
387	if [[ -z "$CONTAINER" ]] && have docker && docker info >/dev/null 2>&1; then
388	  CONTAINER=$(docker ps --format '{{.Names}}' --filter 'name=vllm-qwen36' 2>/dev/null | head -1)
389	  [[ -z "$CONTAINER" ]] && CONTAINER=$(docker ps --format '{{.Names}}' --filter 'name=vllm-' 2>/dev/null | head -1)
390	  [[ -z "$CONTAINER" ]] && CONTAINER=$(docker ps --format '{{.Names}}' --filter 'name=llama-cpp-' 2>/dev/null | head -1)
391	fi
392	
393	# Engine class — drives which probes run inside the container body. Inferred
394	# from container name; user can override with ENGINE_KIND=vllm|llamacpp env var.
395	case "${ENGINE_KIND:-}" in
396	  vllm|llamacpp|unknown) ;;  # respect user override
397	  *)
398	    case "$CONTAINER" in
399	      vllm-*)      ENGINE_KIND="vllm" ;;
400	      llama-cpp-*) ENGINE_KIND="llamacpp" ;;
401	      *)           ENGINE_KIND="unknown" ;;
402	    esac ;;
403	esac
404	
405	if [[ -z "$CONTAINER" ]]; then
406	  echo "_No vLLM or llama.cpp container running. Start one with \`bash scripts/launch.sh\` and re-run for the full report._"
407	else
408	  {
409	    status=$(docker ps --filter "name=$CONTAINER" --format '{{.Status}}' 2>/dev/null | head -1)
410	    ports=$(docker ps --filter "name=$CONTAINER" --format '{{.Ports}}' 2>/dev/null | head -1)
411	    image=$(docker ps --filter "name=$CONTAINER" --format '{{.Image}}' 2>/dev/null | head -1)
412	    echo "- **Name:** \`$CONTAINER\`"
413	    echo "- **Engine:** \`${ENGINE_KIND}\`"
414	    echo "- **Status:** ${status:-unknown}"
415	    echo "- **Ports:** ${ports:-unknown}"
416	    echo "- **Image:** \`${image:-unknown}\`"
417	  } | redact
418	
419	  # Engine-specific probes from this point. vLLM container has Python +
420	  # PyTorch + Genesis markers; llama.cpp container ships a stripped C++
421	  # binary with no Python — different probe set.
422	
423	  # Engine-specific subsections. vLLM container has Python + PyTorch + Genesis
424	  # markers; llama.cpp container ships a stripped C++ binary with no Python
425	  # exec available — different probe set.
426	
427	  if [[ "$ENGINE_KIND" == "llamacpp" ]]; then
428	    # ---- llama.cpp probe set ----
429	    subsection "Container engine state (llama.cpp)"
430	    {
431	      # llama-server prints its version + build flags on startup. Grep the
432	      # boot log for the version banner instead of trying to docker exec
433	      # (the llama-cpp image doesn't ship interactive shell utilities).
434	      llama_version=$(docker logs "$CONTAINER" 2>&1 | grep -E '^build_info:|^version:|^system_info:' | head -3)
435	      if [[ -n "$llama_version" ]]; then
436	        echo "**llama-server version + build:**"
437	        echo '```'
438	        echo "$llama_version"
439	        echo '```'
440	        echo
441	      fi
442	
443	      # Loaded model + ctx + KV type — surfaces model identity from boot log.
444	      model_loaded=$(docker logs "$CONTAINER" 2>&1 | grep -E 'load_model:|llama_model_load_from_file_impl:|llama_kv_cache_init:|llama_init_from_model:' | head -8)
445	      if [[ -n "$model_loaded" ]]; then
446	        echo "**Model load + KV cache init:**"
447	        echo '```'
448	        echo "$model_loaded"
449	        echo '```'
450	        echo
451	      fi
452	
453	      # llama.cpp doesn't have Genesis / vLLM SpecDecoding metrics. Skip
454	      # those grep patterns. Capture warnings/errors only.
455	      boot_errors=$(docker logs "$CONTAINER" 2>&1 | grep -iE '^(warn|error|fatal|abort)|panic|core dumped' | tail -5)
456	      if [[ -n "$boot_errors" ]]; then
457	        echo "**Recent warnings/errors (last 5):**"
458	        echo '```'
459	        echo "$boot_errors"
460	        echo '```'
461	      fi
462	    } | redact
463	
464	    subsection "Full boot log (first 200 lines)"
465	    docker logs "$CONTAINER" 2>&1 | head -200 | redact | details "First 200 lines of docker logs"
466	
467	  else
468	  # ---- vLLM probe set (default for engine=vllm or unknown) ----
469	  subsection "Container Python / CUDA versions"
470	  {
471	    # vLLM version + Torch CUDA build vs host driver mismatch is one of the
472	    # rare failure modes that image SHA pinning doesn't catch. Quick docker
473	    # exec to surface what the container actually sees.
474	    py_versions=$(docker exec "$CONTAINER" python3 -c \
475	      'import torch, sys; print(f"torch={torch.__version__} torch_cuda_build={torch.version.cuda} cudnn={torch.backends.cudnn.version()}")' \
476	      2>&1)
477	    if [[ -n "$py_versions" ]] && [[ "$py_versions" != *"Error"* ]] && [[ "$py_versions" != *"error"* ]]; then
478	      echo "- **PyTorch:** \`${py_versions}\`"
479	    else
480	      echo "- **PyTorch:** (could not query — \`docker exec\` failed or torch not importable)"
481	    fi
482	
483	    vllm_ver=$(docker exec "$CONTAINER" python3 -c 'import vllm; print(vllm.__version__)' 2>&1)
484	    if [[ -n "$vllm_ver" ]] && [[ "$vllm_ver" != *"Error"* ]] && [[ "$vllm_ver" != *"error"* ]]; then
485	      echo "- **vLLM:** \`${vllm_ver}\`"
486	    else
487	      echo "- **vLLM:** (could not query)"
488	    fi
489	
490	    # Container's view of the GPUs — should match host driver, but if NVIDIA
491	    # Container Toolkit is mis-configured this surfaces the mismatch.
492	    cuda_in_container=$(docker exec "$CONTAINER" nvidia-smi --query-gpu=index,name,driver_version --format=csv,noheader 2>&1 | head -4)
493	    if [[ -n "$cuda_in_container" ]] && [[ "$cuda_in_container" != *"Error"* ]] && [[ "$cuda_in_container" != *"command not found"* ]]; then
494	      echo "- **nvidia-smi inside container:**"
495	      echo '  ```'
496	      echo "$cuda_in_container" | sed 's/^/  /'
497	      echo '  ```'
498	    fi
499	  } | redact
500

563	section "Recent failed boot attempts"
564	if ! have docker; then

571	  exited_lines=$(docker ps -a \

583	      finished_at=$(docker inspect "$ex_id" --format '{{.State.FinishedAt}}' 2>/dev/null || echo "")
584	      exit_code=$(docker inspect "$ex_id" --format '{{.State.ExitCode}}' 2>/dev/null || echo "?")

588	      finished_epoch=$(date -d "$finished_at" +%s 2>/dev/null || echo 0)
589	      cutoff_epoch=$(date -d '24 hours ago' +%s 2>/dev/null || echo 0)

594	      # Format relative_when nicely: how many minutes ago?
595	      mins_ago=$(( ($(date +%s) - finished_epoch) / 60 ))

9	# context recall or large prefill activation peaks — for those run

75	detect_engine() {

306	  resp="$(curl -sf -m 120 "${URL}/v1/chat/completions" \
307	    -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
308	    -d "{
309	      \"model\": \"${MODEL}\",
310	      \"messages\": [{\"role\": \"user\", \"content\": \"What is 2+2? One-line answer.\"}],
311	      \"max_tokens\": 4000,
312	      \"temperature\": 0.3,
313	      \"chat_template_kwargs\": {\"enable_thinking\": true}
314	    }")" || { fail "thinking request failed" "Check docker logs"; return 1; }
315	  local analyzed
316	  analyzed="$(echo "$resp" | python3 -c "
317	import sys, json
318	d = json.load(sys.stdin)
319	msg = d['choices'][0]['message']
320	reasoning = msg.get('reasoning') or ''
321	content = msg.get('content') or ''
322	finish = d['choices'][0].get('finish_reason')
323	print(f'{len(reasoning)}|{len(content)}|{finish}|{(reasoning[:60] or \"(empty)\").replace(chr(10), \" \")}|{(content[:60] or \"(empty)\").replace(chr(10), \" \")}')
324	" 2>/dev/null)"
325	  IFS='|' read -r r_len c_len fin r_head c_head <<< "$analyzed"
326	  if [[ -z "$r_len" ]]; then
327	    fail "couldn't parse thinking response" "$(echo "$resp" | head -c 300)"
328	  elif [[ "$r_len" == "0" ]]; then
329	    fail "reasoning field empty (thinking mode didn't engage)" \
330	         "May indicate Genesis Patch 12 didn't land or chat_template_kwargs not honored. content='$c_head'"
331	  elif [[ "$c_len" == "0" ]] && [[ "$fin" == "length" ]]; then
332	    # Reasoning populated but model didn't finish before max_tokens — thinking
333	    # mode is working (reasoning field extracted cleanly), just verbose.
334	    pass "reasoning $r_len chars (model kept thinking, hit max_tokens before finishing — Qwen3.6 is verbose; thinking IS extracting correctly)"
335	    printf "    \033[2mreasoning head:\033[0m %s...\n" "$r_head"
336	  elif [[ "$c_len" == "0" ]]; then
337	    fail "reasoning present but content empty, finish=$fin (not length)" \
338	         "Likely genuine stall — finish_reason should be length if it's just verbosity. reasoning: $r_head"
339	  elif [[ "$r_len" -lt 50 ]]; then
340	    fail "reasoning suspiciously short ($r_len chars)" "reasoning: $r_head"
341	  else
342	    pass "reasoning $r_len chars, content $c_len chars (finish=$fin)"
343	    printf "    \033[2mreasoning:\033[0m %s...\n" "$r_head"
344	    printf "    \033[2mcontent:  \033[0m %s...\n" "$c_head"
345	  fi
346	}
347	run_check "thinking" check_thinking
348	# --------------------------------------------------------------------
349	# 9. Output quality / cascade detection — 2K-token completion, scan
350	#    for the silent <tool_call> inline cascade (MTP × TurboQuant bug)
351	#    and for repetitive degeneracy (stale-draft / sampling collapse).
352	# --------------------------------------------------------------------
353	check_output_quality() {
354	  echo "[7/8] Output quality / cascade detection (2K-token completion) ..."
355	  local resp
356	  resp="$(curl -sf -m 180 "${URL}/v1/chat/completions" \
357	    -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
358	    -d "{
359	      \"model\": \"${MODEL}\",
360	      \"messages\": [{\"role\": \"user\", \"content\": \"Write a detailed 1500-word essay explaining how transformer attention works. Cover: query/key/value projections, scaled dot-product attention, softmax, multi-head attention, positional encodings, and a brief comparison with RNN-based attention.\"}],
361	      \"max_tokens\": 2000,
362	      \"temperature\": 0.6,
363	      \"chat_template_kwargs\": {\"enable_thinking\": false}
364	    }")" || { fail "output quality request failed" "Check docker logs ${CONTAINER}"; return 1; }
365	
366	  local analysis
367	  analysis="$(echo "$resp" | python3 -c "
368	import sys, json, re
369	try:
370	    d = json.load(sys.stdin)
371	    c = d['choices'][0]['message'].get('content') or ''
372	    finish = d['choices'][0].get('finish_reason') or 'n/a'
373	    clen = len(c)
374	    cascade = 'tool_call_cascade' if '<tool_call>' in c else 'none'
375	    # Repetitive cascade: same non-empty line appearing >=5 times consecutively
376	    lines = [l.strip() for l in c.split('\n') if l.strip()]
377	    max_repeat, cur_line, cur_count = 0, '', 0
378	    for l in lines:
379	        if l == cur_line:
380	            cur_count += 1
381	            max_repeat = max(max_repeat, cur_count)
382	        else:
383	            cur_line, cur_count = l, 1
384	    # Lexical variety over the first 200 words (samples coherence)
385	    words = re.findall(r\"[A-Za-z']+\", c.lower())
386	    sample = words[:200]
387	    variety = (len(set(sample)) / len(sample)) if sample else 0.0
388	    print(f'{clen}|{cascade}|{max_repeat}|{variety:.3f}|{finish}')
389	except Exception as e:
390	    print(f'err|{e}|0|0|n/a')
391	" 2>/dev/null)"
392	
393	  IFS='|' read -r clen cascade max_repeat variety finish <<< "$analysis"
394	  if [[ "$clen" == "err" ]]; then
395	    fail "couldn't parse response: $cascade" "$(echo "$resp" | head -c 200)"
396	  elif [[ "${clen:-0}" == "0" ]]; then
397	    fail "empty completion (finish=${finish})" "Likely silent generation failure"
398	  elif [[ "$cascade" == "tool_call_cascade" ]]; then
399	    fail "MTP × TurboQuant cascade — <tool_call> emitted in normal text" \
400	         "Genesis P64/P65 not active or compose using broken MTP path. See README Known issues."
401	  elif [[ "${max_repeat:-0}" -ge 5 ]]; then
402	    fail "repetitive degeneracy — line repeats ${max_repeat}× consecutively" \
403	         "Sampling collapsed (stale-draft? sampler bug?). Check finish_reason=${finish}, vLLM ngram/spec settings."
404	  elif python3 -c "import sys; sys.exit(0 if float('${variety:-0}') >= 0.30 else 1)" 2>/dev/null; then
405	    pass "output OK — ${clen} chars, variety=${variety}, max_line_repeat=${max_repeat}, finish=${finish}"
406	  else
407	    fail "low lexical variety (${variety}, threshold 0.30)" \
408	         "Possible degenerate output. clen=${clen}, finish=${finish}"
409	  fi
410	}
411	run_check "output_quality" check_output_quality
412	
413	# --------------------------------------------------------------------
414	# 10. MTP acceptance length — assert spec-decode is contributing speedup.
415	#     Mean AL >= 2.0 means each step accepts >=1 drafted token on average
416	#     (target_only baseline = 1.0). Production sees AL 3.4-3.8 with n=3.
417	# --------------------------------------------------------------------
418	check_mtp_acceptance() {
419	  echo "[8/8] MTP acceptance length threshold ..."
420	  # Spec-decode metrics extraction is engine-specific:
421	  #   vLLM emits "SpecDecoding metrics: Mean acceptance length: N.NN" to stdout
422	  #   llama.cpp llama-server doesn't emit a "Mean acceptance length" line; spec
423	  #     metrics are inferred from per-slot accept counts in the response timings
424	  #     (engine-internal, not exposed via OpenAI API)
425	  #   SGLang has its own format
426	  # For non-vLLM engines we skip rather than fail — the per-engine spec-decode
427	  # validation is the user's responsibility (e.g. llama.cpp users run their own
428	  # verify-full-mtp.sh adaptations like @lamentofhighborne's, until #87 lands a
429	  # generalized harness).
430	  case "$ENGINE_KIND" in
431	    llamacpp) skip "llama.cpp engine — MTP acceptance check is vLLM-log-format-specific (run engine-side verification separately)"; return 0 ;;
432	    sglang)   skip "SGLang engine — MTP acceptance check is vLLM-log-format-specific";   return 0 ;;
433	  esac
434	  if ! command -v docker >/dev/null 2>&1; then
435	    skip "docker not in PATH (host engine build? — see #87 for generalized harness work)"
436	    return 0
437	  fi
438	  if ! docker inspect "${CONTAINER}" >/dev/null 2>&1; then
439	    skip "container '${CONTAINER}' not found (CONTAINER=none for host endpoints)"
440	    return 0
441	  fi
442	
443	  # Trigger a fresh decode to populate metrics (some vLLM builds only emit
444	  # SpecDecoding stats after a non-trivial generation completes).
445	  curl -sf -m 60 "${URL}/v1/chat/completions" \
446	    -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
447	    -d "{
448	      \"model\": \"${MODEL}\",
449	      \"messages\": [{\"role\": \"user\", \"content\": \"Count from 1 to 80, one number per line.\"}],
450	      \"max_tokens\": 500,
451	      \"temperature\": 0.0,
452	      \"chat_template_kwargs\": {\"enable_thinking\": false}
453	    }" >/dev/null 2>&1 || { fail "metrics-trigger request failed" "Check docker logs"; return 1; }
454	  sleep 3  # let log line flush
455	
456	  local recent
457	  recent="$(docker logs --tail 200 "${CONTAINER}" 2>&1 | grep -iE "SpecDecoding|acceptance length|spec_decode" | tail -3)"
458	  if [[ -z "$recent" ]]; then
459	    skip "no SpecDecoding metrics in logs (compose may not have spec-decode enabled)"
460	    return 0
461	  fi
462	
463	  local al
464	  al="$(echo "$recent" | grep -oiE "(mean acceptance length|acceptance length|al|mean_acceptance_length)[: ]+[0-9]+\.[0-9]+" \
465	        | grep -oE "[0-9]+\.[0-9]+" | tail -1)"
466	  if [[ -z "$al" ]]; then
467	    skip "couldn't parse AL from: $(echo "$recent" | head -c 240 | tr '\n' ' ')"
468	    return 0
469	  fi
470	
471	  if python3 -c "import sys; sys.exit(0 if float('$al') >= 2.0 else 1)" 2>/dev/null; then
472	    pass "MTP acceptance length = ${al} (>=2.0 — spec-decode contributing)"
473	  else
474	    fail "MTP acceptance length = ${al} (<2.0 — spec-decode degraded or off)" \
475	         "Either MTP routing broken (P65 not active?) or accept rate collapsed. Check spec_decode kernel + Genesis env vars."
476	  fi
477	}
478	run_check "mtp" check_mtp_acceptance
479	
480	echo ""
481	if [[ "$FAILED" == "0" ]]; then
482	  printf "\033[32mAll checks passed.\033[0m Stack is ready for full-functionality use.\n"
483	else
484	  printf "\033[31m%d check(s) failed.\033[0m See hints above.\n" "$FAILED"
485	fi
486	
487	if [[ "$RUN_BENCH" == "1" && "$FAILED" == "0" ]]; then
488	  echo ""
489	  echo "=========================================="
490	  echo "  --bench: running scripts/bench.sh"
491	  echo "=========================================="
492	  SCRIPT_DIR="$(cd -- "$(dirname -- "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}")" && pwd)"
493	  URL="${URL}" MODEL="${MODEL}" CONTAINER="${CONTAINER}" \
494	    bash "${SCRIPT_DIR}/bench.sh"
495	fi
496	
497	exit "$FAILED"
